content: I've been trying to recreate some of the German dishes I've discovered since moving to Berlin, but my kitchen renovation is making it difficult.
Ah, the classic renovation challenge! What German dishes have you been wanting to make?
I've been craving spätzle and schnitzel. There's this amazing restaurant near my new place in Kreuzberg that makes the best käsespätzle. I'm determined to master it myself once my kitchen is done.
Kreuzberg is wonderful! Käsespätzle is such comfort food. When is your renovation expected to be complete?
They said six weeks, but we're already at eight. The contractor keeps finding unexpected issues - old wiring, plumbing from the 1970s. Typical Berlin Altbau problems.
That sounds frustrating. At least you're getting a proper update. What's your tech setup like in the meantime? Working from home must be interesting.

I've got my mechanical keyboard collection spread across a folding table in the living room. I'm writing an article about the European tech scene, so I need my specialized keyboards for different writing moods.
A keyboard collection? That's fascinating! How many do you have?

Seven at the moment. My favorite is a custom-built one with silent switches - perfect for late-night writing when my partner is sleeping. I'm actually planning to review keyboards for a tech publication next month.
Seven is quite a collection! Do you find different keyboards suit different types of writing?
Absolutely. For long-form articles, I prefer something with a bit more resistance. For quick emails or notes, I use a lighter touch keyboard. It's become part of my writing ritual.
That's really interesting. I've never thought about keyboards that way. Do you have other rituals that help with your writing process?

Morning birdwatching from my temporary office window helps clear my head. There's a pair of Eurasian blue tits that built a nest in the building's eaves. I've been watching them while I drink my coffee.
How lovely! Nature can be such a good companion during work. Have you always been interested in birds?

Since childhood, actually. My grandmother in Galicia used to take me on walks with her binoculars. She'd teach me the names in both Spanish and Galician. I still remember her excitement when we'd spot something rare.
That's a beautiful memory. It's wonderful that you've maintained that connection. Do you read about birds as well?

I do, but I'm more of a visual person. I prefer watching documentaries or going on birdwatching walks. Though I did just finish a sci-fi novel about a future where birds have evolved to be the dominant species. It was bizarre but fascinating.
That sounds like an unusual premise! What's the title? I might want to check it out.
It's called 'Avian Ascendance' by Mika Chen. Not the best writing, but the concept was so unique I couldn't put it down. Perfect for my commute when I'm not birdwatching out the train window.
I'll have to look that up. It sounds like you've found some wonderful ways to stay engaged while dealing with the renovation chaos.
